hypothesis: Introducing aromatic residues at position 5 and hydrophobic residues at position 11 will enhance SSTR2-specific complementarity in the ECL2/TM5 interface, while reducing off-target affinity to SSTR3/SSTR5 by avoiding generic interactions.

critic_feedback_used.hypothesis: The current hypothesis remains valid: SSTR2 selectivity can be achieved by optimizing non-pharmacophore positions to disrupt off-target interactions. However, structural quality must be improved to increase the pass rate.

proposed_changes.rationale: Reducing the allowed clash score will enforce better backbone and sidechain packing, addressing structural failures observed in the current iteration.
Targeting non-pharmacophore positions for mutagenesis will help disrupt off-target interactions while preserving the critical pharmacophore (FWKT at 7-10 and Cys3/Cys14).
